divergences.decision: dscli is the operator-facing operability surface, and logging is a crosscutting concept whose local files are managed by LogManager under the resolved worker log directory.
divergences.detail: The new dscli logs command hardcodes /opt/yuanrong/datasystem/log/<worker_address>.log. The mental model records dscli as the operability surface and logging as LogManager-managed local files; source confirmation shows worker logs are written under configured/resolved FLAGS_log_dir, with default deployment config ./datasystem/logs, and ordinary worker log names follow log_filename/severity patterns such as datasystem_worker.INFO.log rather than <worker_address>.log. This means the operator command will not read the actual worker logs for normal process deployments and does not respect the logging model.
divergences.evidence: cli/logs.py added in /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff lines 46-47 and 103-105; MM: _mm/socratic/QUESTION_TREE-yuanrong-datasystem.adoc:514-518 and _mm/socratic/QUESTION_TREE-yuanrong-datasystem.adoc:418-421; source: cli/start.py:276-292, cli/deploy/conf/worker_config.json:426-428, src/datasystem/worker/worker.cpp:245-250, src/datasystem/common/log/logging.cpp:157-179, docs/source_zh_cn/appendix/log_guide.md:14-20
divergences.fix: Resolve the worker's actual log_dir/log_filename from the running process command line or config, then tail the current LogManager file pattern under that directory (for example <log_filename>.INFO.log or configured severity files), with tests covering non-default log_dir/log_filename.
